static.description: A polyglot microservices platform for food delivery, built with 5 independent services (API Gateway, Order, Inventory, Driver, Notification) across Kotlin, Java, Python, Go, and TypeScript stacks, communicating via RabbitMQ events and fronted by a JWT-authenticated API Gateway.
domain_concepts: OrderStatus: Enum representing the lifecycle states of an order: PENDING → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY_FOR_PICKUP → IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED, with CANCELLED and REFUNDED as terminal/side states.
OrderItem: An embeddable JPA entity representing a line item within an order, containing menu item ID, name, quantity, and unit price.
MenuItem: A SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant menu item with stock_quantity (-1 means unlimited) and availability tracking.
Driver: A MongoDB document representing a delivery driver with name, phone, vehicle type, availability status, and GPS location.
GeoPoint: A struct/document embedded in Driver storing latitude and longitude coordinates for real-time location tracking.
JwtAuthFilter: A Spring Cloud Gateway global filter that validates JWT tokens and forwards the verified customer ID as an X-Customer-Id header to downstream services.
acquire_lock: A Redis-based distributed lock function in the Inventory Service that prevents concurrent stock overselling by acquiring an exclusive lock on a menu item.
order.events: The RabbitMQ exchange name used for publishing order lifecycle events that the Notification Service consumes.
stripePaymentIntentId: The Stripe PaymentIntent ID (pi_xxxx) stored on an Order, used for manual capture and refund operations.
Restaurant: A SQLAlchemy model representing a restaurant with name, address, cuisine type, rating, and associated menu items.
isValidTransition: A static method on OrderStatus that enforces the allowed state machine transitions for order lifecycle.

dynamic.install_command: cd order-service && mvn dependency:resolve && cd ../inventory-service && pip install -r requirements.txt && cd ../driver-service && go mod download && cd ../notification-service && npm install && cd ../api-gateway && ./gradlew dependencies
dynamic.architecture_summary: The platform follows a microservices architecture with an API Gateway (Kotlin/Spring Cloud Gateway) performing JWT authentication and routing to downstream services. The Order Service (Java/Spring Boot) manages order lifecycle with Stripe payments and publishes events to RabbitMQ, which the Notification Service (Node.js/TypeScript) consumes to send SMS/email via Twilio/SendGrid. The Inventory Service (Python/FastAPI) uses Redis distributed locks for stock reservation, and the Driver Service (Go) tracks driver profiles and locations in MongoDB.

key_components.name: GatewayApplication
key_components.responsibility: Spring Cloud Gateway entry point that routes all external requests to downstream microservices.

key_components.name: JwtAuthFilter
key_components.responsibility: Global gateway filter that validates JWT tokens and injects authenticated customer ID into downstream request headers.

key_components.name: OrderServiceApplication
key_components.responsibility: Spring Boot entry point for the Order Service managing order lifecycle, payments, and event publishing.

key_components.name: OrderController
key_components.responsibility: REST controller exposing endpoints for creating, listing, updating, and cancelling orders.

key_components.name: OrderService
key_components.responsibility: Core business logic for order creation with Stripe payment, status transitions with validation, and RabbitMQ event publishing.

key_components.name: Order
key_components.responsibility: JPA entity representing a customer order with line items, delivery address, payment intent, and lifecycle status.

key_components.name: OrderStatus
key_components.responsibility: Enum defining valid order lifecycle states and transition rules between them.

key_components.name: StripePaymentGateway
key_components.responsibility: Live Stripe SDK implementation for creating payment intents and processing refunds.

key_components.name: inventory-service/main.py
key_components.responsibility: FastAPI application handling restaurant/menu queries and stock reservation with Redis distributed locks.

key_components.name: models.py
key_components.responsibility: SQLAlchemy ORM models for Restaurant and MenuItem entities with stock management semantics.

key_components.name: driver-service main.go
key_components.responsibility: Go HTTP service managing driver profiles, availability, and GPS location tracking backed by MongoDB.

key_components.name: notification-service index.ts
key_components.responsibility: Node.js/TypeScript service consuming RabbitMQ order events and dispatching SMS (Twilio) and email (SendGrid) notifications with idempotency.

agent_safe_operations: Add new REST endpoints to existing service controllers/handlers
Add new unit tests or integration tests to any service
Modify business logic in OrderService, inventory main.py, or driver handlers
Add new domain models or DTOs
Add new Python dependencies to requirements.txt
Add new Go dependencies via go get
Add new npm dependencies to notification-service/package.json
Refactor internal service code without changing API contracts
Add new notification channels in notification-service
agent_forbidden_operations: Modify database migration files or auto-generated schema DDL
Change JWT secret or authentication bypass logic in JwtAuthFilter
Modify CI/CD workflow files without explicit approval
Change the openapi.yaml contract without coordinating across services
Remove or weaken OrderStatus transition validation
Disable or bypass Redis distributed locking in inventory service
Commit real API keys or secrets to any file
potential_pitfalls: Each service uses a different language and build tool — running tests requires Java 21 + Maven, Python 3.11 + pip, Go 1.21, and Node.js 20 + npm all available
The inventory service uses async SQLAlchemy with asyncpg driver — the database_url must use postgresql+asyncpg:// scheme, not plain postgresql://
Order status transitions are strictly validated in OrderStatus.isValidTransition — invalid transitions throw IllegalStateException, not silently ignored
Redis distributed locks in inventory service have a 10-second TTL with 40 retries — long-running test scenarios may hit lock timeouts
The API Gateway's JwtAuthFilter runs at order -100, meaning it executes before routing — any new public paths must be added to PUBLIC_PATHS
The notification service uses an in-memory Set for idempotency — this is explicitly noted as needing Redis in production, so don't rely on it across restarts
The driver service uses MongoDB ObjectID (not UUID) for driver identifiers, unlike other services which use UUID — JSON field mapping differs (camelCase vs snake_case in BSON)
Docker Compose env vars like REDIS_HOST reference container names (e.g., 'redis', 'postgres') — running services locally requires overriding these to 'localhost'
The order-service uses Lombok annotations (@Getter, @Setter, @Builder) — builds will fail without Lombok annotation processor configured
The api-gateway uses Gradle (build.gradle.kts) while order-service uses Maven (pom.xml) — don't confuse build commands between these Java/Kotlin services
